The package provides declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, exposing resource types including `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, and more.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Examples section is completely empty** — no code samples showing how to create a `MySqlFlexibleServer` or any child resources (databases, firewall rules, etc.)\\n- **Authenticate the Client section** has the heading but no content — should explain that deployment is done via `azd` and point to provisioning docs\\n- **Next steps section is empty** — no links to related packages, REST API docs, or Azure portal\\n- **Key concepts section lacks resource descriptions** — lists no individual resource types or their purpose; the library exposes 11+ provisionable resource classes with no guidance on which to use\\n- **No sample test files exist** — `tests/` contains only a `.csproj`; there are no `Samples/` files with snippet-backed examples that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can inject into the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`\\n\\nFill in the empty/incomplete sections:\\n\\n**Authenticate the Client section** — replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nThis library uses the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) to deploy the declarative infrastructure you define. There is no runtime client authentication required — provisioning resources is done via `azd up` or similar commands. For more information see the [Azure Developer CLI documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n**Key concepts section** — replace the current one-liner with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\nKey resource types provided:\\n\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — The primary resource representing an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server. Configure SKU, version, storage, backup, high availability, and network settings.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — A database hosted on a Flexible Server.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — A firewall rule controlling network access to a Flexible Server.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — A server-level configuration parameter.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — An Azure AD administrator account for a Flexible Server.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** — Represents a server backup.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Scheduled maintenance window configuration.\\n- **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Threat protection settings for a Flexible Server.\\n```\\n\\n**Examples section** — replace the empty section with a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server with a Database and Firewall Rule\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**Next steps section** — replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Learn about the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore related provisioning packages: [`Azure.Provisioning`]((www.nuget.org/redacted), [`Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault`]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. 20\\n                },\\n                Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n                {\\n                    BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n                    GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n                },\\n                HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability\\n                {\\n                    Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled\\n                }\\n            };\\n            infra.Add(server);\\n\\n            MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new(\\u0026#34;mysqlDb\\u0026#34;)\\n            {\\n                Parent = server,\\n                Charset = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4\\u0026#34;,\\n                Collation = new(\\u0026#34;allowAzureServices\\u0026#34;)\\n            {\\n                Parent = server,\\n                StartIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;,\\n                EndIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n            infra.Add(firewallRule);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet details:**\\n- Snippet name: The package provides declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, exposing resource types including `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, and more.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Examples section is completely empty** — no code samples showing how to create a `MySqlFlexibleServer` or any child resources (databases, firewall rules, etc.)\\n- **Authenticate the Client section** has the heading but no content — should explain that deployment is done via `azd` and point to provisioning docs\\n- **Next steps section is empty** — no links to related packages, REST API docs, or Azure portal\\n- **Key concepts section lacks resource descriptions** — lists no individual resource types or their purpose; the library exposes 11+ provisionable resource classes with no guidance on which to use\\n- **No sample test files exist** — `tests/` contains only a `.csproj`; there are no `Samples/` files with snippet-backed examples that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can inject into the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`\\n\\nFill in the empty/incomplete sections:\\n\\n**Authenticate the Client section** — replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nThis library uses the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) to deploy the declarative infrastructure you define. There is no runtime client authentication required — provisioning resources is done via `azd up` or similar commands. For more information see the [Azure Developer CLI documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n**Key concepts section** — replace the current one-liner with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\nKey resource types provided:\\n\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — The primary resource representing an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server. Configure SKU, version, storage, backup, high availability, and network settings.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — A database hosted on a Flexible Server.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — A firewall rule controlling network access to a Flexible Server.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — A server-level configuration parameter.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — An Azure AD administrator account for a Flexible Server.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** — Represents a server backup.\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Scheduled maintenance window configuration.\\n- **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Threat protection settings for a Flexible Server.\\n```\\n\\n**Examples section** — replace the empty section with a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server with a Database and Firewall Rule\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**Next steps section** — replace the empty section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Learn about the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore related provisioning packages: [`Azure.Provisioning`]((www.nuget.org/redacted), [`Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault`]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Provisioning;\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.MySql;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Category(\\u0026#34;Samples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void CreateBasicMySqlFlexibleServer()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic\\n            Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n            MySqlFlexibleServer server = new(\\u0026#34;mysqlServer\\u0026#34;, MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n            {\\n                AdministratorLogin = \\u0026#34;mysqladmin\\u0026#34;,\\n                Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021,\\n                Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n                {\\n                    Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2ads_v5\\u0026#34;,\\n                    Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose\\n                },\\n                Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage\\n                {\\n                    StorageSizeInGB = 20\\n                },\\n                Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n                {\\n                    BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n                    GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n                },\\n                HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability\\n                {\\n                    Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled\\n                }\\n            };\\n            infra.Add(server);\\n\\n            MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new(\\u0026#34;mysqlDb\\u0026#34;)\\n            {\\n                Parent = server,\\n                Charset = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4\\u0026#34;,\\n                Collation = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4_unicode_ci\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n            infra.Add(database);\\n\\n            MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new(\\u0026#34;allowAzureServices\\u0026#34;)\\n            {\\n                Parent = server,\\n                StartIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;,\\n                EndIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n            infra.Add(firewallRule);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet details:**\\n- Snippet name: `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic`\\n- Test file path: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`\\n- README location: under `## Examples`, the placeholder `` ```C# 2026-04-09T16:44:39.6188699Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-04-09T16:44:39.6188851Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-09T16:44:39.6189056Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-09T16:44:39.6189380Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-04-09T16:44:39.6189490Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
